meaning: vt.生染,就原料染色,使根深蒂固adj.生染的,根深蒂固的n.生染的纤维,原料染色,本质
examples: Instead, the new habits we deliberately ingrain into ourselves create parallel pathways that can bypass those old roads.
Training camp, he said, will quickly ingrain those principles.
Ingrain the idea in your mind that NOTHING will get you where you want to be other than hard work.
Many are also now starting to ingrain this into their organizational framework and business processes.
Because the more we continue to make the same mistakes, the more we ingrain the ineffective behaviors into our lives.
